title: What to do as the world falls apart: A framework for action
content: From the archive | Having spent twenty years articulating the more-than-human predicament, Nate Hagens shares a timely first-pass framework for action and response organized around what to do now, which could be applied in various contexts and at multiple scales.

creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Across Africa, farmers are adopting regenerative agricultural practices that support food sovereignty amid global instability
content: As geopolitical tensions drive up resource costs and disrupt supply, fertilizer prices may soar, endangering farming systems dependent on imports. Regenerative solutions, like green manure and community finance, are expanding across African countries, restoring local control over food systems.

creator: Kate Schecter

title: On Natural Capital: The Value of the World Around Us by Partha Dasgupta – review
content: In On Natural Capital, Partha Dasgupta argues that mainstream economics has failed to recognize the finite nature of the natural world. Blending ecology and economics, he sets out a framework to account for our impact on the environment.
snippet: In On Natural Capital, Partha Dasgupta argues that mainstream economics has failed to recognize the finite nature of the natural world. Blending ecology and economics, he sets out a framework to accou
creator: Jacob Lea
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Bill Rees: A childhood moment on a Canadian farm led to ecological footprint analysis
content: A childhood moment on a Canadian farm shaped Bill Rees’s understanding of ecological limits, leading to the development of ecological footprint analysis and decades of warnings about global overshoot.
snippet: A childhood moment on a Canadian farm shaped Bill Rees’s understanding of ecological limits, leading to the development of ecological footprint analysis and decades of warnings about global overshoot.
creator: Robert Jensen
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: There’s no single path through collapse. It spans multiple systems and perspectives
content: In his new book, the author argues that without a clearer view of the systems we’re embedded in, as well as our cultural and historical contexts, our responses to the polycrisis will continue to fall short.
snippet: In his new book, the author argues that without a clearer view of the systems we’re embedded in, as well as our cultural and historical contexts, our responses to the polycrisis will continue to fall
creator: Juan Pablo Quiñonez
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The strange genius—and limits—of living beings
content: A reflection on animal behaviour, evolutionary feedback systems and why living beings can appear both extraordinarily intelligent and strangely “dumb” depending on context.
snippet: A reflection on animal behaviour, evolutionary feedback systems and why living beings can appear both extraordinarily intelligent and strangely “dumb” depending on context.
creator: Tom Murphy
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Toxic dust from the shrinking Salton Sea is harming children’s lung growth amid water loss, study finds
content: A new study shows that toxic dust from Southern California’s shrinking Salton Sea impedes lung development in children, as water loss and industrial expansion reshape the region’s environment.
snippet: A new study shows that toxic dust from Southern California’s shrinking Salton Sea impedes lung development in children, as water loss and industrial expansion reshape the region’s environment.
creator: Jill Johnston
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The climate crisis is becoming a legal obligation, not a political choice
content: A landmark International Court of Justice advisory opinion has clarified that states have a duty to prevent climate harm, marking a shift toward enforceable climate accountability and strengthening the emerging framework of ecological and rights-based environmental law.
snippet: A landmark International Court of Justice advisory opinion has clarified that states have a duty to prevent climate harm, marking a shift toward enforceable climate accountability and strengthening th
creator: Erika Schelby
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Human nature didn’t create the polycrisis. Our systems did – and they can be redesigned
content: The drivers behind the polycrisis, including relentless extraction, extreme inequality, and environmental degradation, are often attributed to human nature, but evidence suggests they are products of historically conditioned systems.
snippet: The drivers behind the polycrisis, including relentless extraction, extreme inequality, and environmental degradation, are often attributed to human nature, but evidence suggests they are products of
creator: Jeremy Lent
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Rethinking our place in nature means rethinking the law
content: As Indigenous knowledge gains recognition and environmental crises deepen, a growing movement argues that granting legal rights to nature can protect it from exploitation.
snippet: As Indigenous knowledge gains recognition and environmental crises deepen, a growing movement argues that granting legal rights to nature can protect it from exploitation.
creator: Erika Schelby
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Solar panels aren’t as “clean” as we like to think
content: Solar power has enabled off-grid living and low-carbon energy, but its industrial supply chains and large-scale rollout come with environmental costs we cannot ignore.
snippet: Solar power has enabled off-grid living and low-carbon energy, but its industrial supply chains and large-scale rollout come with environmental costs we cannot ignore.
creator: Kollibri terre Sonnenblume
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Without pluralism within the climate movement, we risk handing the future to the far right
content: The climate crisis demands urgency, not ideological uniformity. In an already fragmented movement, requiring adherence to specific positions on issues beyond climate action deepens division and opens space for anti-democratic influence.
snippet: The climate crisis demands urgency, not ideological uniformity. In an already fragmented movement, requiring adherence to specific positions on issues beyond climate action deepens division and opens
creator: Vlad Vexler
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How Pacific communities use sea worms to track time and seasonal shifts through a changing climate
content: In the Pacific Islands, the annual spawning of palolo sea worms feeds communities, marks time and sustains cultural traditions. Why this Indigenous tradition is becoming increasingly important as climate change intensifies weather events.
snippet: In the Pacific Islands, the annual spawning of palolo sea worms feeds communities, marks time and sustains cultural traditions. Why this Indigenous tradition is becoming increasingly important as clim
creator: Katarina Zimmer
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Strait of Hormuz reopens for now, but global supply chains remain at risk
content: Carrying about 20% of the world’s traded oil and gas, the Strait of Hormuz is a critical global chokepoint. Even if it remains open, restoring full energy and material flows will take time, with ongoing consequences to global supply systems.

creator: Chris Rhodes
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The growing push to grant legal rights to nature
content: From Ecuador to New Zealand and India, a growing number of countries are recognizing the legal rights of nature, though not without legal and political challenges.
snippet: From Ecuador to New Zealand and India, a growing number of countries are recognizing the legal rights of nature, though not without legal and political challenges.
creator: Erika Schelby
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Nuclear safety at risk: what’s changing under Donald Trump
content: As sweeping deregulation accelerates under Donald Trump, long-standing nuclear safety regulations are being rolled back with little public scrutiny, raising new concerns about risks to both human health and the environment.
snippet: As sweeping deregulation accelerates under Donald Trump, long-standing nuclear safety regulations are being rolled back with little public scrutiny, raising new concerns about risks to both human heal
creator: Kirsten Stade
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The empty desk policy: Why remote work is the sustainability win we’re ignoring
content: The daily commute is more than an inconvenience; it is a major source of pollution and wasted time. As return-to-office demands grow, remote work may be one of the most overlooked tools for reducing emissions while improving quality of life and accessibility.

creator: Saskia Karges
